facts.text: Liberty Corner began as Annin's Corner, named for early settler John Annin, and was renamed during the Revolution when residents raised a liberty pole.
In August 1781, French troops under General Rochambeau camped at Bullion's Tavern in Liberty Corner before marching toward Yorktown.
The Liberty Corner Presbyterian Church, founded in 1837, and the volunteer Fire Company, established in 1910, showcase the village’s enduring community spirit.
The Liberty Corner Historic District entered the National Register of Historic Places in 1991, honoring architecture from 1865 to 1935.
Located roughly six kilometers south of Bernardsville, Liberty Corner maintains a rural atmosphere even though it sits near New York City via I‑287 and I‑78.
Scottish immigrant John Annin settled the area in 1722, purchasing 270 acres of fertile farmland that would become the heart of Liberty Corner.
